provisions.description: Prohibits DoD funds from being used for new production not funded in FY2025, increased production rates above FY2025 levels, or initiation/resumption of any project/activity for which appropriations were not available in FY2025.

provisions.description: Sets the CR expiration: funds are available until the earliest of (1) enactment of specific appropriation, (2) enactment of applicable FY2026 appropriations Act without provision for the project, or (3) January 30, 2026.

notes: Standard CR boilerplate requiring most limited funding action

provisions.description: Entitlements and mandatory payments whose budget authority was in the appropriations Acts referenced in section 101, and Food and Nutrition Act activities, shall continue at program-level rates under current law through January 30, 2026. Obligations for mandatory payments due on or about the first day of any month through 30 days after the CR expiration may continue.

notes: Standard CR boilerplate for mandatory payment continuation; subsection (b) allows continuation of monthly mandatory payments beyond CR expiration

provisions.description: Prohibits use of federal funds to initiate, carry out, implement, or notice a reduction in force (RIF) in any federal department, agency, or office during the CR period. Applies to all civilian positions regardless of type or funding source. Exceptions for voluntary separations, court orders, and orderly shutdown during a lapse. Retroactively nullifies any RIF proposed, noticed, or executed between October 1, 2025 and enactment; requires restoration of affected employees with backpay.
provisions.raw_text: Notwithstanding section 106(1), during the period between the date of enactment of this Act and the date specified in section 106(3) of this Act, no f
notes: MAJOR POLICY RIDER: Prohibition on reductions in force with retroactive effect
Subsection (a) prohibits RIFs during CR period
Subsection (b) applies to all civilian positions regardless of funding source
Subsection (c) provides exceptions for voluntary separations, court orders, orderly shutdown during lapse
Subsection (d) defines RIF as actions under 5 U.S.C. 3501-3504, 3595, or similar reductions
Subsection (e) retroactively nullifies any RIF between Oct 1, 2025 and enactment; requires reinstatement, backpay, and agency notification within 5 days

cross_references.description: Senior Executive Service reduction in force

provisions.description: Amends Section 297A of the Agricultural Marketing Act of 1946 to redefine 'hemp', creating new categories of 'industrial hemp' and 'hemp-derived cannabinoid product'. Narrows the definition of hemp to exclude synthetic cannabinoids, products containing cannabinoids synthesized outside the plant, intermediate hemp-derived cannabinoid products marketed directly to consumers, and final products containing more than 0.4 mg combined total THC and similar cannabinoids per container. Effective 365 days after enactment.
provisions.policy_area: Agriculture - Hemp regulation and intoxicating hemp products
provisions.title: VII
provisions.raw_text: Effective 365 days after the enactment of this Act, Section 297A of the Agricultural Marketing Act of 1946 (7 U.S.C. 1639o) is amended—
notes: Major policy rider closing the 'intoxicating hemp' loophole from the 2018 Farm Bill
Creates new 'industrial hemp' definition for non-cannabinoid uses (fiber, grain, seed, microgreens, research)
Sets 0.4 mg per container combined THC limit for final hemp-derived cannabinoid products
Excludes from hemp definition: (1) synthetic cannabinoids, (2) semi-synthetic cannabinoids manufactured outside the plant, (3) intermediate products marketed to end consumers
Excludes FDA-approved drugs from the definition of hemp-derived cannabinoid product
365-day delayed effective date allows industry transition

cross_references.target: Section 297A of the Agricultural Marketing Act of 1946 (7 U.S.C. 1639o)
cross_references.description: Redesignates paragraphs, strikes and replaces definition of hemp with expanded definitions

provisions.description: Requires the Food and Drug Administration, in consultation with other relevant Federal agencies, to publish within 90 days: a list of all cannabinoids naturally produced by Cannabis sativa L. as reflected in peer-reviewed literature; a list of all THC-class cannabinoids naturally occurring in the plant; a list of other cannabinoids with similar effects to THC-class cannabinoids; and additional information about the defined term 'container'.
provisions.title: VII
provisions.raw_text: Within 90 days of the enactment of this act, the Food and Drug Administration, in consultation with other relevant Federal agencies, shall publish—
notes: Paragraph (3) of SEC. 781, a standalone directive separate from the hemp definition amendment
Note: the hemp definition amendment in paragraphs (1)-(2) has a 365-day delayed effective date, but this directive is 90 days from enactment
Lists required will inform implementation of the new hemp-derived cannabinoid product exclusions

cross_references.description: Definition of 'container' referenced in subparagraph (D) of this directive

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary of Agriculture to provide written notification to House and Senate Committees on Appropriations no fewer than 3 business days before terminating any grant, cooperative agreement, or contract award totaling $1,000,000 or more from funds in this Act or any previous Act, including details on recipient, amount, fiscal year, account, program/project/activity, title, and detailed justification for termination.
provisions.title: VII
provisions.raw_text: The Secretary of Agriculture shall provide written notification to the House and Senate Committees on Appropriations no fewer than 3 business days in ad
notes: Applies to termination of awards totaling $1,000,000 or more
Covers funds from this Act or any previous Act — broad retroactive scope
3 business day advance notification requirement constrains agency action
Required notification content: recipient, amount, fiscal year, account, program/project/activity, award title, and detailed justification
Provision appears responsive to concerns about executive branch termination of existing awards

provisions.description: Amends Section 10 of the Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 2005 (2 U.S.C. 6628) to require notification of Senate offices when legal process seeks disclosure of Senate data, prohibits nondisclosure orders from barring such notification, creates a private cause of action for Senators with statutory damages of $500,000 per instance, waives sovereign immunity, establishes special rules for target vs. non-target investigations, and applies retroactively to events on or after January 1, 2022.
provisions.title: II
provisions.raw_text:
(a) 
In general
Section 10 of the Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 2005 (2 U.S.C. 6628) is amended—
(1) subsection (a)— (A) by redesignating p
notes: Extensive amendment to Senate data protection law
Defines 'covered data', 'Senate data', 'target of a criminal investigation'
Requires providers and the Office of the SAA to notify Senate offices of legal process seeking Senate data
No court order may bar notification; no liability for providing notice
Creates private cause of action: $500,000 statutory damages per instance or actual damages (whichever greater), plus attorney's fees
Waives sovereign immunity; bars assertion of qualified or absolute immunity
Affirmative defense available for target investigations with proper court authorization
5-year statute of limitations from actual notice
Retroactive to January 1, 2022; for pre-enactment violations, 5-year period runs from date of enactment
Delayed notice for target investigations requires court order, limited to 60-day periods, renewable

cross_references.target: Section 10 of the Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 2005 (2 U.S.C. 6628)
cross_references.description: Senate data notification and protection requirements

provisions.description: Prohibits use of funds for research commencing on or after July 1, 2025, using canines, felines, or non-human primates unless the Secretary of Veterans Affairs personally approves and certifies the research meets strict criteria including that no alternatives exist, research is combat-related, and ethical considerations are addressed. Mandates elimination of such research by September 20, 2026.

notes: Secretary must personally certify: (A) no alternatives exist, (B) research is combat-related per 10 U.S.C. 1413(e), (C) consistent with Dec 15, 2017 canine research policy, (D) ethical considerations evaluated

30-day advance report to Appropriations Committees required before new research commences (subsection (c))

provisions.raw_text: Except as otherwise provided in this section and the amendments made by this section, notwithstanding any other provision of law, the authorities (incl
notes: Subsection (a). Blanket extension of all authorities provided by or amended by the Agriculture Improvement Act of 2018 (the 2018 Farm Bill), including mandatory programs at current funding levels.
Continues authorities as in effect on September 30, 2025, including as modified by section 4101 of division D of the American Relief Act, 2025 (P.L. 118-158).

Extension runs to the later of: (A) September 30, 2026; (B) the date specified in the 2018 Farm Bill or as amended by P.L. 119-21, the Expanding Public Lands Outdoor Recreation Experiences Act (P.L. 118-234), and other post-2018 laws; or (C) the date under P.L. 118-158 section 4101.
Subject to numerous exceptions in subsection (e).

notes: Subsection (c)(2). Critical farm bill provision that prevents permanent law (Agricultural Adjustment Act of 1938, Agricultural Act of 1949, etc.) from taking effect.

provisions.program_name: Over-the-Counter Monograph Drug User Fee Program
provisions.statutory_reference: Section 744M of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (21 U.S.C. 379j-72)
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text: Section 744M(a)(1) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (21 U.S.C. 379j–72(a)(1)) is amended— (1) subparagraph (A)—
notes: Comprehensive reauthorization of OTC monograph drug user fee authority for FY2026-2030

Subsection (a) amends fee assessment: changes applicable period for determining which facilities are subject to fees; updates cessation-of-activity cutoff dates; establishes new fee due date schedule with special installment provisions for FY2026 and FY2027

Subsection (b) rewrites fee revenue amounts: establishes formula based on annual base revenue plus inflation adjustment, operating reserve adjustment, direct cost adjustments, additional amounts ($2,373,000 for FY2026, $1,233,000 for FY2027, $854,000 for FY2028), and a potential one-time facility fee workload adjustment

Subsection (c) amends adjustments: updates inflation adjustment formula, fixes operating reserve at 10 weeks, sets direct cost adjustments ($135,000 for FY2026, $300,000 for FY2027, $55,000 for FY2028, $30,000 for FY2029, $0 for FY2030), adds new one-time facility fee workload adjustment for FY2028-2030 triggered by average facility count exceeding 1,625 with less than 30% arrears rate, and renumbers annual fee setting provision
Subsection (d) extends crediting and availability of fees from 'FY2021 through FY2025' to 'FY2026 through FY2030'

notes: Amends subsection (a): restructures performance reporting, replaces reference to CARES Act section 3861(b) with reference to section 6502 of the OTC Monograph Drug User Fee Amendments

Adds new paragraph (2) requiring additional reporting beginning FY2026 on: Tier 1/Tier 2/specified safety/GRASE finalization order request processing; average processing timelines; postmarket safety activities; facility registration and fee payment; topical drug evidence/testing standards implementation; and progress on nonclinical testing alternatives to animal testing for sunscreen active ingredients
Adds confidentiality protections under new paragraph (3)
Extends sunset from 2025 to 2030 in subsection (d)
Adds new paragraph (4) requiring public availability of robust written minutes of all FDA-industry negotiation meetings within 30 days

provisions.description: Amends Section 505G of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to add new subsection (r) establishing evidence and testing standards for active ingredients for topical administration, including allowance for real world evidence, non-animal testing methods, and guidance requirements.
provisions.policy_area: FDA regulation of nonprescription topical drugs
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 6506. 
Treatment of active ingredients for topical administration
(a) 
In general
Section 505G of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (21 U.S.C. 355h) is amended by adding at the end the following:
notes: Subsection (a) adds new subsection 505G(r) with three paragraphs: (1) evidence standards allowing real world evidence, (2) non-animal testing methods for topical ingredients with guidance due within 1 year, (3) clarification that existing GRASE standards are not altered

Subsection (b) sets requirements for the final administrative order on sunscreen active ingredients under CARES Act section 3854, requiring consideration of historical safety data, skin cancer prevention role, and new testing standards

cross_references.target: Section 505G of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (21 U.S.C. 355h)
cross_references.description: Adds new subsection (r) on evidence and testing standards for topical active ingredients

provisions.description: Amends Section 505(b) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to add new paragraph (7) establishing procedures and guidance for Rx-to-nonprescription (OTC) switch applications, including pre-submission meetings, guidance on evidentiary standards due within 18 months, stakeholder engagement plan due within 1 year, and definitions for full and partial switches.
provisions.title: V
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 6507. 
Increasing the clarity and predictability of the process for developing applications for Rx-to-nonprescription switches

(a) 
In general
Section 505(b) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (21 U.S.C. 355(b)) is amended by adding at the end the following:
notes: Subsection (a) adds new 505(b)(7) with subparagraphs (A) through (E): meetings, guidance within 18 months, stakeholder engagement plan within 1 year, definitions of full and partial switch, and rule of construction
Defines 'Rx-to-nonprescription switch' to include both full switches (drug moves entirely to OTC) and partial switches (some conditions OTC, others remain Rx)

cross_references.target: Section 505(b) of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (21 U.S.C. 355(b))
cross_references.description: Adds new paragraph (7) on Rx-to-nonprescription switches

provisions.description: Requires the Comptroller General to submit a report within 1 year evaluating Rx-to-nonprescription switch applications, including number approved since October 1, 2022, average review times, barriers to timely review, FDA stakeholder engagement, and opportunities for CMS collaboration on claims data analysis.
provisions.title: V

(b) 
GAO Report

In general

Effective date
The amendments made by this title shall take effect on October 1, 2025, or the date of the enactment of this Act, whichever is later, except that fees under part 10 of subchapter C of chapter VII of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic
notes: Ensures no gap in fee assessment authority by mandating fee assessment from October 1, 2025 regardless of when the bill is enacted

provisions.title: VI
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 6601. 
Extending availability of funding for No Surprises Act implementation
Section 118(a) of division BB of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2021 (Public Law 116–260) is amended
notes: Amends Section 118(a) of division BB of P.L. 116-260 in three ways: (1) restructures the existing text to create a numbered paragraph (1), (2) extends the availability of existing funding from September 30, 2025 to January 30, 2026, and (3) adds a new paragraph (2) providing $14,000,000 in additional funding to the Secretary of Health and Human Services for the period October 1, 2025 through January 30, 2026.
The $14,000,000 is described as 'in addition to amounts otherwise appropriated under paragraph (1)', making it supplemental to the existing No Surprises Act implementation funding.

provisions.description: Makes multiple substantive amendments to the VA Partial Claim Program under section 3737 of title 38, including: (a) clarifying relationship to Secretary's other powers under section 3720(h); (b) revising administration of partial claims including lien calculation, extending the timeline from 120 to 180 days, and amending guaranty and liquidation rules; (c) requiring loan holders to service the loan; (d) revising default and foreclosure provisions including borrower liability, administrative costs, and non-judicial sale procedures; (e) authorizing the Secretary to issue administrative guidance in advance of regulations for the Partial Claim Program and loss mitigation options under section 3732(d).
provisions.policy_area: Veterans housing loan programs — Partial Claim Program
provisions.title: III
provisions.raw_text:
Improvements to Partial Claim Program of the Department of Veterans Affairs

(a) 
Clarification of relationship to other powers of Secretary
Sect
notes: Subsection (a) restricts Secretary's purchase of loans under 3732(a)(2) unless consistent with 3732(d)
Subsection (b) changes partial claim subordination basis and extends timeline from 120 to 180 days
Subsection (c) requires loan holder to service the loan after partial claim
Subsection (d) makes borrowers liable for losses on defaulted partial claims and allows non-judicial sale to discharge partial claim interest
Subsection (e) allows Secretary to issue guidance before formal regulations for both the Partial Claim Program and loss mitigation options

cross_references.target: Section 3720(h) of title 38, United States Code
cross_references.description: Clarifies relationship to loan purchase power under 3732(a)(2)

cross_references.target: Section 3737 of title 38, United States Code
cross_references.description: Multiple amendments to Partial Claim Program administration, default, and foreclosure provisions

cross_references.target: Section 3732(d) of title 38, United States Code
cross_references.description: Loss mitigation options referenced in guidance authority
Non-judicial sale provision overrides requirement in 28 U.S.C. 2410

provisions.description: Requires the Comptroller General of the United States to submit annual reports to the Senate and House Committees on Veterans' Affairs on the performance of the VA Partial Claim Program, including data on partial claims filed and approved, redefault and foreclosure rates, comparison with other loss mitigation options, housing loan volume, denied applications, refinances, and delinquency rates. Also requires GAO to conduct and report on an assessment of the benefits and challenges of the Partial Claim Program not later than one year before it terminates.
deadlines: Not later than one year after enactment and annually thereafter (annual reports)
Not later than one year before the Partial Claim Program terminates (assessment report)
provisions.title: III
provisions.raw_text: Not later than one year after the date of the enactment of this Act, and every year thereafter until the Partial Claim Program terminates, the Comptrol
notes: Annual reports cover: partial claims filed/approved, redefault/foreclosure rates, comparison with other loss mitigation options, housing loan volume, denied applications, refinancing data, and delinquency rates (60+ and 90+ days late), all disaggregated by quarter

Assessment must consider borrower characteristics, loan performance after loss mitigation, cost comparison, similarities/differences with COVID-19 VA Partial Claim Payment program, lessons learned, and comparison with other federal housing agencies' partial claim programs

cross_references.target: Section 3737 of title 38, United States Code
cross_references.description: Defines the Partial Claim Program that is the subject of the reports

cross_references.target: Chapter 37 of title 38, United States Code
cross_references.description: VA housing loan guaranty program referenced for loan data

SEC. 5002(a) is a sweeping blanket extension of all Agriculture Improvement Act of 2018 authorities (the 2018 Farm Bill) through at least September 30, 2026, with mandatory programs funded at current levels. The CBO cost of this extension depends on baseline mandatory spending projections.
SEC. 5002(e) carves out numerous mandatory funding provisions from the blanket extension, effectively allowing those specific funding streams to expire. This likely serves as a budget offset.
SEC. 5002(c)(2) suspending permanent price support authorities is essential to prevent reversion to Depression-era commodity programs.
SEC. 5002(g) applies retroactively to September 30, 2025 to prevent any gap in authorities.
